summary: Russian forces launched a massive combined air and drone strike targeting Kyiv, Dnipro, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia and other regions, with over 700 aerial weapons reported. Ukrainian air defenses shot down 642 targets, including 40 rockets and 602 UAVs, while civilian infrastructure suffered casualties and fires. Drone activity continues over multiple fronts, with Shahed attacks reported near Kyiv, Bucha, Irpin, and other locales.
key_points: Massive combined strike by Russian forces hit Kyiv, Dnipro, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, causing building fires and power outages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
Ukrainian air defense downed 642 aerial targets, including 40 rockets and 602 UAVs, across Kyiv and other regions (Source: @kpszsu/63655).

Shahed UAVs detected over Kyiv, Bucha/Irpin/Gostomel, Borispol, and Lukiantsi, indicating continued low‑altitude attacks (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67449, @mon1tor_ua/67448, @mon1tor_ua/67427, @mon1tor_ua/67459).
Two MiG‑31K fighters relocated from Olenya to Savasleka airfield; potential Kinjal missile launches noted (Source: @eRadarrua/95026).
Ukrainian forces reported advances of up to 500 m in Sumy direction and engagements near Shostka, Ivolzhansky, and Pisarevsky (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
Ukrainian civilian casualties: three injured in a multi‑storey building in Slavyansk‑on‑Kuban, and a child killed in Dnipro after a Russian strike (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605, @UkraineNow/81609).
Ukrainian defense industry sites in Khmelnytskyi and Kyiv (Ukroboronprom) were hit and set on fire (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30611, @ukr_leaks_eng/30607).

sections.executive_summary: Massive Russian combined air and drone strike hit Kyiv, Dnipro, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia, igniting buildings and causing power outages. Ukrainian air defenses reported shooting down 642 aerial targets, including rockets and Shahed UAVs. Drone activity persists across multiple fronts, with Shahed attacks near Kyiv and other cities.

points: Ukrainian forces engaged in offensive actions in the Sumy direction, advancing up to 500 meters in Shostka district and fighting in Ivolzhansky and Pisarevsky areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
In the Dnipropetrovsk region, a child born in 2023 was recovered from rubble after a Russian strike that raised civilian deaths to eight (Source: @UkraineNow/81609).
Ukrainian defense industry facilities in Khmelnytskyi region were set on fire following Russian attacks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30611).
Ukrainian air defense units reported destroying 642 aerial targets, including 40 rockets and 602 UAVs, across Kyiv and other regions (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
Ukrainian troops reported expansion of control in Kupyansk built‑up area despite increased enemy UAV activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
Ukrainian forces in the Dobropolsky direction continue slow advances, consolidating front lines near Grishino, Mirnye, Vasilyevka, and Shevchenko (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
Ukrainian air force reported no signs of Tu‑95MS or Tu‑160 sorties, with only Tu‑22M3 and MiG‑31K posing potential threats (Source: @eRadarrua/95040).

Russian Armed Forces conducted a combined strike with drones and missiles on Kyiv, Dnipro, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia, igniting a Ukroboronprom building and an energy facility (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
In the Krasnodar region, a UAV attack caused a fire at the Ilskoy refinery and debris fell on a multi‑storey building in Slavyansk‑on‑Kuban (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
Crimea and Sevastopol repelled a serious night attack, according to regional reports (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
More than ten UAVs were destroyed in Taganrog and four districts of Rostov region, indicating intensive Russian UAV deployment (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
Russian forces launched 33 Shahed‑type attack UAVs from multiple launch areas including Bryansk, Kursk, and Crimea (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
Russian missile launches included 8 anti‑ship Circon missiles from Crimea, Kursk and other regions (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the north‑east and east directions, warning of possible air strikes near front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/63671).
Ukrainian units of the "North" Group engaged in firefights near Bachivsk and surrounding areas on the Sumy direction (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
Assault units advanced up to 400 meters on the Volchansk sector, fighting in the village of Okhrikovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
Russian UAV attacks hit civilian targets in Kursk region, including a car in Shchekino village, causing a civilian death (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
In the Kharkiv direction, Ukrainian forces advanced toward the logistics hub village of Kachiy Lopan', with three enemy soldiers surrendering (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
Ukrainian air defenses reported intercepting Shahed UAVs over the eastern regions, including attacks from launch points in Bryansk and Kursk (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
Ukrainian forces reported continued pressure on Kostyantynivka, with red zone expansion south of Illinivka (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547).
Ukrainian troops noted enemy UAV activity over the east, with multiple Shahed attacks directed toward Kyiv and surrounding oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
Shahed UAV detected over Borispol heading toward Kyiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67448).
Two Shahed UAVs approached Kyiv from the north (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67459).
Ukrainian air defense reported downing numerous UAVs in the night of 2 June, with attacks originating from Bryansk, Kursk, and other northern launch sites (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
Ukrainian forces reported engagements near Shostka district in Sumy direction, indicating northern front activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
Russian tactical aviation activity warned for north‑east direction, suggesting possible air strikes (Source: @kpszsu/63671).
Ukrainian defense reported a Shahed attack on Bucha/Irpin/Gostomel area (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67449).
Ukrainian forces observed enemy UAVs heading toward Chernihiv region, specifically towards Slavutych (Source: @kpszsu/63697).
Shahed UAVs launched from Kursenivka toward Lukiantsi/Shulyavka in the south (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67427).
Ukrainian defense reported a Shahed attack on the Black Sea coast heading toward Mykolaiv region (Source: @kpszsu/63669).
Ukrainian forces noted UAV activity from the Black Sea heading toward Odesa region (Source: @kpszsu/63698).
Russian missile strikes targeted Zaporizhzhia region, part of the combined strike on southern oblasts (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
Ukrainian air defense reported intercepting Shahed UAVs over the southern front, including attacks from the Black Sea launch area (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
Ukrainian forces reported ongoing pressure on Kostyantynivka, with red zone expansion south of Illinivka (Source: @DeepStateUA/23547).
Ukrainian troops engaged enemy forces near the village of KomsoMolskoe in the east of Zaporizhzhia region, indicating southern‑eastern activity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
Ukrainian air defense shot down 642 aerial targets, including 40 rockets and 602 UAVs, during a massive combined attack (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
Russian forces launched 33 Shahed‑type attack UAVs and multiple cruise and ballistic missiles from various launch areas (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
Two MiG‑31K fighters were airborne, relocating from Olenya to Savasleka, with potential Kinjal missile launches anticipated (Source: @eRadarrua/95026).
No signs of Tu‑95MS or Tu‑160 sorties were observed; only Tu‑22M3 and MiG‑31K posed potential threats (Source: @eRadarrua/95040).
Ukrainian air force conducted over 1,089 sorties in May, providing air cover and fire support (Source: @kpszsu/63683).
Russian tactical aviation activity warned for north‑east and east directions, indicating possible air strike threats (Source: @kpszsu/63671).
Ukrainian forces reported downing of over 70,000 aerial targets in May, including 5,053 Shahed UAVs (Source: @kpszsu/63683).

key_developments: Russian combined drone and missile strike hit Kyiv, Dnipro, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia, igniting a Ukroboronprom building and causing power outages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
Ukrainian air defenses downed 642 aerial targets, including 40 rockets and 602 UAVs, across multiple regions (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
Shahed UAVs were detected over Kyiv from the north and over Bucha/Irpin/Gostomel, indicating continued low‑altitude attacks (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67459, @mon1tor_ua/67449).
Two MiG‑31K aircraft were in flight, relocating from Olenya to Savasleka, with potential Kinjal missile launches noted (Source: @eRadarrua/95026).
Ukrainian forces advanced up to 500 m in the Sumy direction and engaged enemy units near Shostka and Ivolzhansky (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605).
Ukrainian defense industry sites in Khmelnytskyi and Kyiv were hit and set on fire by Russian attacks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30611, @ukr_leaks_eng/30607).
Russian tactical aviation activity warned for north‑east and east fronts, suggesting possible air strike threats (Source: @kpszsu/63671).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are employing a high‑intensity combined air and UAV campaign, targeting strategic infrastructure in Kyiv and major cities while launching Shahed swarms from multiple launch zones. Ukrainian air defenses are proving effective, intercepting the majority of rockets and drones, but the volume of attacks sustains pressure on civilian populations and critical facilities. Continued MiG‑31K activity and potential Kinjal missile launches indicate a persistent threat from high‑speed, high‑altitude platforms.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is reinforcing air defense assets across all fronts, with notable successes in downing UAVs and missiles, while also conducting limited offensive advances in Sumy and Kupyansk areas. Russian regional commands report successful strikes on industrial targets and claim repelling attacks in Crimea and Sevastopol, suggesting a focus on both offensive and defensive postures. International observers note civilian casualties and infrastructure damage, raising concerns over escalation.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Shahed launch points in Bryansk, Kursk, and Crimea remains a priority, as does tracking MiG‑31K flight paths for potential Kinjal deployment. The fire at the Ukroboronprom building and Khmelnytskyi defense industry sites may impact Ukraine's production capacity. Ukrainian advances in the Sumy direction could create opportunities for further pressure on Russian supply lines, while the high volume of aerial attacks underscores the need for sustained air defense resupply.
